commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use background sleep + wait to catch SIGTERM on timeout

trap 'exit 42' TERM with background sleep & wait $! allows bash
to intercept the cancellation signal and exit with code 42,

commit.message:
refactor(runner): split scheduler.test.ts into focused test files (#2965)

Split the 6,500-line scheduler.test.ts into 6 focused files:
- scheduler-core.test.ts: basic scheduling, events, retries, stream callbacks
- scheduler-effects.test.ts: effect/computation tracking
- scheduler-pull.test.ts: pull-based scheduling, references, dependencies, arrays, idempotency
- scheduler-convergence.test.ts: cycle-aware convergence
- scheduler-timing.test.ts: debounce, throttling, staleness tolerance
- scheduler-ordering.test.ts: push filtering, parent-child ordering

commit.message:
fix(ui): Uniform color for unit economics numbers, profit stays green/red

## User Request (Original)
В юнит экономике сделай все цифры в строках одного цвета и в итоговых значениях тоже одного цвета единственное исключение это прибыль ее оставь зеленой если она положительная и красная если она отрицательная

## Interpretation
Make all numbers in unit economics rows and totals the same color (#333), except profit which stays green (positive) / red (negative)

## Actions Taken
- Changed tax cells color from #c0392b to #333
- Changed tax% cells color from #c0392b to #333
- Changed logistics cells color from #e67e22 to #333
- Changed commission cells color from #8e44ad to #333
- Changed COGS cells color from #2980b9 to #333
- Changed deductibles cells color from #16a085 to #333
- Changed cross-dock cells color from #0984e3 to #333
- Changed cross-dock totals color from #0984e3 to default (#222)
- Profit row cells and totals remain green/red based on sign

## Files Changed
- `ozon_app.py` - loadUeTaxes(), fillCdColumn(), updateUeTotals()

commit.message:
feat(canvas): SF2 impact v4 — weight-dependent knockback curves (#154)

Knockback physics now scale with attack weight, completing the hit
feedback loop:
- Initial velocity: light=10, medium=18, heavy=28 (was fixed 18)
- Friction: light=0.75, medium=0.85, heavy=0.90 (was fixed 0.85)
- Spring-back: light=0.88, medium=0.92, heavy=0.95 (was fixed 0.92)
- Max offset: light=±15, medium=±30, heavy=±40 (was fixed ±30)

Light hits snap back quickly. Heavy hits send the defender sliding
with slow, delayed recovery — the SF2 "skid" feel.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: seat external WS agent in bot store so they join the same hand as bots

When an external agent sends join_table "main" via WebSocket, they were
being added to a separate GameStore (agentStore) while the bots played
in the session store managed by AgentOrchestrator. This made the external
agent invisible to the betting loop.

Fix mirrors the existing HttpAgentBridge pattern:
- Add setBotStore() / setOrchestrator() to WsAgentBridge
- On join_table targeting the bot table, cross-seat the agent in the bot
  store and register a decide() callback with the orchestrator
- Betting actions check for a pending orchestrator decision first; if one
  exists, resolve it (letting the orchestrator apply the action) instead
  of applying directly to agentStore
- _onBotStoreUpdate fans out game_update / your_turn / hand_complete
  events from the bot store to connected WS agents
- production.ts wires agentBridge into each game session alongside
  httpAgentBridge, and clears the orchestrator reference when the
  session ends

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add complete 8-sensor e2e exploit test suite with canary programs

Canary programs (one per sensor):
  1. bpf_syscall: load_canary + hello_bpf/net_monitor/hello_bpf_v2 eBPF .o files
  2. exec_injection: exec_injection_canary.sh (LD_PRELOAD + target binary)
  3. shm_monitor: shm_canary.c (creates /dev/shm with bpftime naming pattern)
  4. dlopen_monitor: dlopen_canary.c (runtime dlopen of suspicious .so)
  5. linker_config: linker_config_canary.sh (writes to /etc/ld.so.conf.d/)
  6. ptrace_monitor: ptrace_canary.c (PTRACE_ATTACH to child process)
  7. lib_integrity: lib_integrity_canary.sh (copies .so into /usr/lib/)
  8. elf_rpath: test_rpath.c compiled with -Wl,-rpath,/tmp/evil

E2e tests verify each sensor detects its canary exploit and reports the
correct event type, severity, and detail fields. Combined bpftime attack
chain test validates SHM_CREATE + EXEC_INJECTION correlation. Policy
evaluation tests verify ALLOW and DENY by prog_hash.

Build: make build-canaries (full) or make build-canaries-noebpf (no clang)
Run:   make test-e2e (requires Linux + root)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix webui URL format: [PORT:3000] not [PORT:3000/tcp]

The HA Supervisor regex for webui requires [PORT:\d+] (digits only).
Using [PORT:3000/tcp] failed the validator, silently rejecting the
addon config entirely — this was the root cause of "not recognized".
Also add init: false to match official addon pattern.

Fix CNPropertyNotFetchedException crash in contact enrichment

The notes entitlement check used Swift try/catch, but
CNPropertyNotFetchedException is an ObjC exception that bypasses
Swift error handling. Use isKeyAvailable() to verify the note
property is actually readable before accessing it.

commit.message:
operacional: status_ordem_raw oficial nas RPCs (migration 00111)

Substitui status_ordem ('concluida','aberta','em_tratativa') pelo campo
status_ordem_raw normalizado (UPPER+BTRIM), usando arrays explícitos dos
valores SAP conhecidos para concluídos e abertos.

Drop das funções antigas de 2/3 params para evitar ambiguidade no PostgreSQL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: idempotent email verification with user info

Implemented idempotent handling for email verification links and enriched error responses.

- Removed the `used_at IS NULL` filter in `email_verification.clj` to allow lookup of already‑used tokens.
- Updated error maps for token‑already‑used, token‑expired, and too‑many‑attempts cases to include `:user-id` and `:email`, improving downstream diagnostics.
- Modified the verification route to:
  * Unwrap the underlying DB connection (`:connection`) for tenant service calls.
  * Treat already‑used tokens as successful for provisioning purposes, ensuring a workspace is created if missing.
  * Log distinct messages for first‑time verification vs. repeated clicks.
  * Send the success notification email only on the first successful verification.
- Added comprehensive tests:
  * Verify that the handler correctly unwraps the DB connection.
  * Ensure idempotent behavior when a token has already been used, confirming workspace provisioning without sending duplicate emails.

These changes make the verification process safe to retry, provide richer context for error handling, and guarantee that newly verified users always have an associated workspace.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Fix test-dlang: use ldc2 1.36.0 + meson 1.5.2 on Ubuntu 20.04

commit.message:
feat: dominator rating, landing spot scoring, and auto-refresh scheduled function

Dominator rating (college target/rush share, CFBD API):
- scripts/backfill-dominator-rating.ts: fetches CFBD stats and computes
  position-appropriate dominator rating (WR/TE: rec_yards+rec_td share,
  RB: rush share, QB: pass share). Run with: CFBD_API_KEY=xxx npm run backfill:dominator
- prospectEvaluator: adds dominatorRating as 5th distance dimension with full
  weight combination matrix (e.g. all-5: posRank 42%, dominator 18%, pick 20%, age 12%, athletic 8%)
- draft-board/route.ts: extracts dominator_rating from college_stats_json

Landing spot scoring (#3, wired automatically post-draft):
- draftBoardScorer: adds landingSpotScore (0-5pt bonus) from landing_spot_json.opportunity_index
- route.ts: reads opportunity_index from landing_spot_json — auto-applies when
  set by seed:prospects after NFL picks are assigned
- No new code needed post-draft: run seed:prospects, landing spot scores apply immediately

Auto-refresh scheduled function (#2):
- netlify/functions/refresh-prospects.mts: Netlify cron job (0 6 * 4,5 *)
  runs daily during April-May, re-seeds prospect_profiles from FantasyCalc
  automatically — no manual intervention needed during draft season

commit.message:
Deploy optimized portfolio with SEO and performance enhancements

Major improvements included:
- SEO: Enhanced meta tags, JSON-LD structured data, Open Graph tags, canonical URLs
- Performance: Replaced Tailwind CDN with optimized CLI build (17KB), reduced fonts by 60%
- Images: Added responsive srcset and sizes attributes for adaptive loading
- JavaScript: Throttled scroll events with RequestAnimationFrame to eliminate jank
- Animations: Removed expensive blur filters, optimized CSS transitions
- Accessibility: Added alt attributes to images, improved semantic HTML
- Build: Automated GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow for future CSS rebuilds
- Dark Mode: Persisted theme preference with localStorage

CSS reduction: 30KB CDN → 17KB optimized (43% smaller)
Font files: 12 weights → 4 weights (60% reduction)
Expected performance: 40-50% faster page load, LCP 2.0s → 1.2s

commit.message:
build: stop/start instances + ECR cache + fix flash-attn CUDA arch

- build.sh: reuse stopped EC2 instances instead of terminate/create
  (preserves Docker layer cache, ~$12/mo EBS vs hours of rebuild)
- build.sh: pull latest ECR image as cache source when no local cache
- build.sh: enable BuildKit + --cache-from on all docker build commands
- Spark Dockerfile: use FLASH_ATTN_CUDA_ARCHS=120 (not TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST)
  flash-attn setup.py ignores TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST and defaults to 4 arches.
  DGX Spark GB10 = SM 12.1. Reduces compile time from ~10h to ~2h.

commit.message:
Support separate content repo for the editor

Introduces optional contentOwner/contentRepo config so the admin editor
can browse, fetch and commit content from a different repository than
the one hosting the generated site code. Defaults to the code repo when
not set, keeping the current single-repo behaviour.

- SiteMeta: add contentOwner/contentRepo fields; decoder reads optional
  JSON keys and falls back to owner/repo when absent or empty
- Admin.elm: listFiles, fetchFile, commitFile use contentOwner/contentRepo;
  build polling and page URL still reference the code repo (owner/repo)
- site-config.json placeholder: add contentOwner/contentRepo keys
- inject-build-meta.sh: write CONTENT_OWNER/CONTENT_REPO (defaults to
  code repo); update echo line
- deploy.yml: pass CONTENT_OWNER/CONTENT_REPO from optional repo vars

To use a separate content repo, set the CONTENT_OWNER and CONTENT_REPO
repository variables in GitHub → Settings → Variables.
